title: Novavax Announces License Agreement with Pfizer for Matrix-M Adjuvant Technology
text: Novavax entered into a license agreement with Pfizer for use of Novavax's Matrix-M adjuvant on January 20, 2026. Under the terms of the agreement, Pfizer will obtain a non-exclusive license for Matrix-M for use with Pfizer's products in up to two disease areas. Novavax will receive an upfront payment of $30 million and the potential to receive up to $500 million in development and sales milestones. Additionally, Novavax is eligible to receive tiered high mid-single digit percentage royalty payments on sales of any product by Pfizer that includes Matrix-M. Pfizer will be solely responsible for the development and commercialization of its products utilizing Matrix-M and Novavax will be responsible for the supply of Matrix-M. This partnership has the potential to generate billions of dollars of revenue for Novavax over the life of the agreement. Matrix-M is a saponin-based adjuvant that enhances vaccine immune responses. With these agreements in place, partners have the right to evaluate Matrix-M in over 30 unique fields of experimentation targeting more than 50% of the projected over $100 billion market for infectious disease and oncology vaccines and immunotherapeutics. This partnership represents Novavax's expanding strategy to license its proprietary technology platform to major pharmaceutical partners.
